revisedPrompt:
Generate exactly one PNG image: a crisp 16-bit SNES-style pixel-art terrain tile sheet for game development.

HARD OUTPUT CONTRACT:
- One image only.
- Exact canvas: 64x64 px.
- Grid: 4 rows x 4 columns, 16 frames total.
- Each tile/cell: exactly 16x16 px.
- Fully opaque PNG. No transparency.
- No visible grid lines, no borders, no tile IDs, no labels, no text, no watermark.
- Every frame must stay inside its own 16x16 cell and maintain the same scale.
- Solid #FF00FF magenta fills only genuinely empty/background space, but tiles should mostly fill each cell edge to edge where appropriate.

STYLE:
- Bright mythical-Japan satoyama palette, cheerful warm golden-hour light.
- Hand-painted Ghibli-like feeling translated into crisp low-resolution pixel art.
- Top-left light source, lower-right shadow.
- Consistent palette, readable fine pixel clusters, no antialiasing blur.
- Use a dark one-pixel outline where useful.
- Top-down RPG/world-map terrain, NOT isometric, no vanishing point.

SUBJECT:
A grassy earthen embankment autotile set, replacing stone walls in natural zones. It must communicate a clear vertical height drop using three visual layers wherever an edge or corner face appears:
A) Top lip: bright warm-lit grass fringe with tiny clover-like flower pixels and rounded overhanging tufts.
B) Vertical face: oblique earthen slope with drooping grass blades, exposed dark soil, pale roots, small mossy stones, and strong 3D shading. It must read as a forward-facing vertical surface, not flat top-down ground.
C) Base shadow: dark contact shadow on the lower ground edge, softened with sparse pixels.

LAYOUT, STRICT 4x4:
Row 1, cells 1-4: center/high plateau grass surface variants. Flat high-ground grass, soft painterly texture, broad color drift, low-frequency natural patches, matches warm satoyama grass hue. No wall face in these four center tiles.
Row 2, cells 1-4: cardinal edge tiles in order N, S, W, E. Each shows the three-layer stack: top grass lip, earthen vertical face, base shadow. The S-edge tile is the hero frontal embankment descending toward the viewer, with the clearest full face.
Row 3, cells 1-4: rounded organic outer corner tiles in order NW, NE, SW, SE. Two embankment faces meet in each tile, but the turn is curved and natural, not a square 90-degree corner. Grass blades, roots, stones, and shadow wrap around the rounded corner.
Row 4, cells 1-4: accent tiles: 1 mossy rock embedded in the slope, 2 wildflower clump at the grass lip, 3 trailing vine on the earthen face, 4 bare-earth patch with pebble scatter. Keep all accents compatible with the same embankment autotile identity.

TILEMAP QUALITY:
- Avoid checkerboard or repeated square-stamp feeling.
- Vary edge breakup, grass tufts, soil clusters, root pixels, moss stones, flower pixels, and lighting while keeping the full set cohesive.
- Transition edges should blend naturally with grass and earth, not form hard rectangular bands.
- World-map scale: large readable geographic terrain, not close-up village-ground detail.

AVOID:
No characters, no animals, no bird or companion creature, no logos, no emblems, no modern objects, no weapons, no armor, no helmets, no ninja/samurai imagery, no horror mood, no dark gloomy palette. Do not imitate Mario, Nintendo, Zelda, Final Fantasy, Pokemon, Dragon Quest, Secret of Mana, Okami, or Sakuna characters, logos, or likenesses.
